{"step_by_step_thinking": "Mannitol, methazolamide, and clonidine are all known to decrease intraocular pressure in glaucoma. Mannitol is an osmotic diuretic that reduces the volume of aqueous humor, methazolamide is a carbonic anhydrase inhibitor that decreases aqueous humor production, and clonidine is an alpha-2 agonist that reduces aqueous humor production. Dexamethasone, on the other hand, is a corticosteroid that can cause an increase in intraocular pressure, especially in susceptible individuals. Therefore, dexamethasone is the exception.", "answer_choice": "D"}
